name: smooth-the-video-editor-20260406-0728
idea: Smooth the video editor layout transition when panes lock/unlock. Currently locking the generations pane causes an instant jump from standard layout (stacked preview+timeline) to condensed layout (side-by-side) because line 148 of VideoEditorShell.tsx does  — the isGenerationsPaneLocked flag instantly flips the layout. Fix: (1) Remove isGenerationsPaneLocked from the condensed calculation — let the ResizeObserver naturally detect the height change as LayoutMainContent animates the bottom padding over 300ms. (2) Add CSS transitions to the grid layout change so the preview/timeline panels animate their size change smoothly instead of jumping. The container already has willChange and transition classes. (3) Keep it lightweight — no layout animation libraries, just CSS transitions on the grid template rows and opacity/transform on the panels swapping. Key file: src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx. The ResizeObserver on line 141 already handles tooSmall detection, so removing the isGenerationsPaneLocked override lets it work naturally with the animated padding.

last_gate.rationale: Clean first iteration with zero unresolved flags. The plan is well-scoped: 3 surgical edits to one file (remove isGenerationsPaneLocked from 3 lines), plus lightweight CSS transitions. No new dependencies, no architecture changes, no cross-cutting concerns. The assumptions are sound — the ResizeObserver already handles tooSmall detection, and the LayoutMainContent padding animation provides the natural trigger. The three open questions (debouncing, hysteresis, Back button) are reasonable runtime concerns but are best validated during execution rather than requiring plan iteration.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Weighted score is 0 with no flags raised. Preflight passed on all checks. No recurring critiques apply — this plan touches only VideoEditorShell.tsx layout detection, which has no overlap with any escalated debt subsystem. The plan's scope (~20 lines changed) is proportionate to the fix.
warnings: If the ResizeObserver fires multiple times during the 300ms padding animation causing rapid condensed/standard flipping, add a small debounce (100-150ms) to the tooSmall state setter during execution.
Verify the Back button behavior when condensed is triggered by pane lock vs forceCondensed — the plan assumes this is acceptable but it should be confirmed in the smoke test.

settled_decisions.decision: Remove isGenerationsPaneLocked from condensed calculation entirely; rely on ResizeObserver tooSmall detection driven by LayoutMainContent's animated padding.
settled_decisions.rationale: The ResizeObserver already exists and handles the same threshold logic. The boolean shortcut was causing the instant jump by bypassing the animation.

settled_decisions.decision: CSS transitions only (transition-opacity on layout containers, transition-[grid-template-rows] on standard layout grid). No animation libraries.
settled_decisions.rationale: The two layouts are different React subtrees so grid property transitions between them aren't possible. Opacity crossfade is the lightest viable approach.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: First iteration; follow gate recommendation: PROCEED.
